people.name: Ngoc Han Nguyen
people.role: Assistant Professor in Human Resource Management
people.expertise: Highly skilled migrant employment, global talent management, flexible employment, and innovation.
people.otherPositions: Lecturer at University of Economics Ho Chi Minh City (on leave) External researcher at Statistics Netherlands HR ambassador in the HR division of the Academy of Management (AOM)

people.name: Miriam Arnold
people.expertise: My research explores the pivotal role of leadership in fostering employee health and resilience amid workplace challenges. I am particularly interested in the intricacies of work design, including the cognitive appraisal of job stressors and the determinants of resource value. I aim to capture these dynamic processes with intensive longitudinal data collections. Additionally, I conduct meta-analyses and systematic reviews on these topics to consolidate and advance our understanding. Keywords: leadership, employee health, resilience, work design, cognitive appraisal, job stressors, resources, longitudinal research, meta-analyses, systematic reviews.

people.name: Stefan Berger
people.expertise: I am an Associate Professor of Organizational Behavior at the University of Groningen and hold (executive) lecturer positions at the University of St. Gallen and the University of Bern. I research, teach, and consult on how organizations can design and lead work in ways that enable effective and sustainable collaboration.
people.otherPositions: Teacher (University of St.Gallen) Teacher (University of Bern)

people.name: Jessica de Bloom
people.expertise: My research focuses on vanishing boundaries between work- and non-work life as one aspect of today´s rapidly changing working conditions. I investigate the impact of these changes on employee well-being and performance as well as the role of modern information and communication technology. My aim is to generate knowledge to understand topical societal phenomena and to bridge the scientist-practitioner gap by communicating scientific knowledge to a wide audience of scientists from various disciplines, professionals working in the field, students, and wider society. Keywords: stress, occupational health, work-related well-being, recovery, leisure, work, non-work, leisure crafting, job crafting, creativity, work engagement, diary studies, longitudinal research, ecological momentary assessments
people.otherPositions: - Academy of Finland Research Fellow (Tampere University, Finland) - Advisory board member for research project conducted by the German Federal Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (BAUA)

people.name: Carsten de Dreu
fields: Micro-economics, Industrial Organization and Game Theory
people.expertise: My research concerns the mechanisms and functions of cooperation, creativity, and conflict within and between groups. The approach is broadly interdisciplinary, covering the behavioral sciences (e.g., sociology and social psychology), business and economics (e.g., organizational behavior, experimental economics), and the biological sciences (e.g., neurobiology, evolutionary theory). Indeed, while most work considers these dynamics in humans, some of it also considers other animal species, non-human primates in particular. Theory is tested with laboratory experiments, evolutionary agent-based simulations, and quantitative reviews and (meta)analyses.
people.otherPositions: Board Member NWO (Domein SGW) Research Affiliate, German Primate Center, Gottingen (Germany) Research Affiliate, Institute for Advanced Study, University of Amsterdam
